name: cairn
description: Local hybrid index for things you intentionally collect — code, docs, web pages, PDFs, raw text. FTS5 + vector embeddings + AST knowledge graph in a single sqlite file. v1.2 ships explicit safety gates: path allowlist (CAIRN_ALLOWED_ROOTS), ingest size caps, and CAIRN_OFFLINE for air-gapped deployment. v1.2.1 adds a startup warning when no allowlist is set and publishes GGUF SHA256s for pre-cached air-gapped deployment. MCP server lets models query and maintain a curated local knowledge base. No telemetry, no API keys, no accounts.

env.name: CAIRN_RUNTIME
env.description: 'ollama' (default — requires localhost ollama with nomic-embed-text pulled) or 'embedded' (in-process via node-llama-cpp; auto-downloads GGUFs to ~/.cairn/models on first use, ~785 MB; download is blocked if CAIRN_OFFLINE=1).

env.name: CAIRN_CHAT_MODEL
env.description: Override the default doc-extraction chat model. Default: hf.co/unsloth/Qwen3-0.6B-GGUF:UD-Q8_K_XL. Doc-extraction is skipped silently if the chat model isn't reachable; chunk indexing keeps working either way.

env.name: CAIRN_CPU_ONLY
env.description: Set to 1 to force CPU-only inference on the embedded runtime. Useful when sharing a GPU with another model.

env.name: CAIRN_DEBUG_DOC
env.description: Set to any truthy value to log per-doc extraction counts (concepts/edges/dropped) during cairn add / refresh.

env.name: CAIRN_OFFLINE
env.description: v1.2+. Set to '1' or 'true' to block all non-localhost network egress: web fetch (no `cairn add <url>`) and Hugging Face GGUF auto-download (embedded runtime requires pre-cached models via absolute modelPath). Localhost ollama is NOT blocked. Use for air-gapped or strict-egress deployments.

env.name: CAIRN_ALLOWED_ROOTS
env.description: v1.2+. Comma-separated list of absolute paths. When set, `cairn add` rejects any local-path ingestion (kind: code|file|pdf) outside these roots. Trailing slashes normalized. Defense-in-depth for MCP-connected agents that might be prompt-influenced into indexing the wrong place; real protection is host-side per-call approval. Unset = no restriction.

env.name: CAIRN_MAX_INGEST_FILES
env.description: v1.2+. Default 10000. Pre-check on `addCode`: aborts before any chunking/embedding if the directory's file count exceeds this. Bypassable via CLI `--force`; MCP intentionally has no force option (host-side per-call approval is the override).

env.name: CAIRN_MAX_INGEST_BYTES
env.description: v1.2+. Default 524288000 (500 MB). Pre-check on `addCode`: aborts before any work if total bytes exceed this. Same bypass model as the file cap.

install.label: Install cairn (also bundled in lib/cairn/). Pinned to exact version.

external.name: ollama
external.description: Required for the default runtime. Run `ollama serve` and `ollama pull nomic-embed-text`. Optionally `ollama pull hf.co/unsloth/Qwen3-0.6B-GGUF:UD-Q8_K_XL` for doc-extraction concept/edge/tag emission. Skip entirely by setting CAIRN_RUNTIME=embedded (which then needs Hugging Face access for the first-use GGUF download, unless CAIRN_OFFLINE=1 is set with pre-cached models).

binaries.name: cairn
binaries.description: CLI for ingestion, retrieval, and admin: add / list / search / ask / graph / path / tags / refresh / reindex / link / unlink / links / remove / init.

binaries.name: cairn-mcp
binaries.description: Stdio MCP server. Exposes the read + ingest surface so models can both query and maintain a curated local knowledge base. Mutating ops (remove / link / unlink / reindex) are intentionally CLI-only and not exposed via MCP.

actions.name: CAIRN_SEARCH
actions.description: Hybrid chunk search (FTS5 + vector, RRF-fused). Params: query, k?, kind?, source?, tag?. Returns ranked chunks with source/file/line/score.

actions.name: CAIRN_LIST
actions.description: List indexed sources. Params: kind?. Returns SourceRow[].

actions.name: CAIRN_GRAPH
actions.description: Entity-level retrieval. Params: query? xor entity_id?, k?, tag?. Returns entities + outbound/inbound edges + tags.

actions.name: CAIRN_ASK
actions.description: Composed retrieval: search hit + per-hit entity context (entities in the hit's file + their 1-hop neighbors + tags) in one call. Params: query, k?, kind?, source?, tag?, maxEntitiesPerHit?, maxEdgesPerEntity?.

actions.name: CAIRN_PATH
actions.description: Shortest path between two entities via BFS over the edge graph (batched layer fetch). Params: from, to, maxDepth?, directed?. Returns path or null.

actions.name: CAIRN_TAGS
actions.description: List every tag in use across active entities, with counts. Discovery surface for the --tag filter on search/ask/graph.

actions.name: CAIRN_ADD
actions.description: Ingest a new source. Auto-detects kind: URLs → web, directories → code, *.pdf → pdf, other files → file. Params: kind?, target, label?, include?, exclude?. Subject to CAIRN_ALLOWED_ROOTS (path allowlist) and CAIRN_MAX_INGEST_FILES / CAIRN_MAX_INGEST_BYTES (size caps) when set. The MCP `force` option is intentionally absent — host-side per-call approval is the override path.

actions.name: CAIRN_REFRESH
actions.description: Re-index an existing source. Fetches latest content, re-chunks and re-embeds if changed. Use 'all' to refresh every source. Params: ref.

trust_model.summary: You trust what you index. Cairn does not auto-crawl. Every source enters via an explicit `cairn add` (CLI, library, or MCP) by a user or an agent the user explicitly authorized for that call.
trust_model.autonomous_invocation: Blocked via disable-model-invocation: true. The model cannot autonomously call cairn tools — every call requires explicit user invocation through the host. User-initiated flows ('index this repo for me', 'find related online files') work normally because the user's request is the explicit invocation context.
trust_model.mcp_access: When invoked, MCP gives the connected agent read + ingest access — that's what MCP is. The host (Claude Desktop, OpenCode, etc.) controls which agents connect AND (per disable-model-invocation: true) gates each call behind explicit user approval. Cairn assumes both gates are honored host-side.
trust_model.isolation_recommendation: For sensitive content, run cairn against a different `dbPath` to physically isolate the index. The single-file sqlite design makes this trivial.

safety.autonomous_invocation_blocked_note: disable-model-invocation: true. Tool calls require explicit user invocation through the host. Matches the conservative default used by torch.market and other side-effect-bearing skills. The user's 'I tell the agent to index X' / 'I tell the agent to find related online files' flow still works because the user's request to the agent IS the explicit invocation context; what's blocked is the model autonomously deciding to call cairn without being asked.

network_egress.summary: Three categories of outbound traffic, all bounded.

categories.name: user-initiated web fetch
categories.trigger: explicit `cairn add <url>` (CLI / library / MCP CAIRN_ADD with kind=web)

categories.name: localhost ollama
categories.trigger: default runtime (CAIRN_RUNTIME unset or =ollama); embed + optional chat HTTP calls to 127.0.0.1:11434
categories.blocked_by: switch to CAIRN_RUNTIME=embedded; not affected by CAIRN_OFFLINE because localhost is not internet egress

categories.name: Hugging Face GGUF download
categories.trigger: first use of CAIRN_RUNTIME=embedded when the requested model is not in ~/.cairn/models cache
categories.blocked_by: CAIRN_OFFLINE=1 (forces fail-fast); pre-cache models on a connected machine and reference them via absolute `modelPath`

path_allowlist.description: Comma-separated absolute paths. When set, `cairn add` rejects any local-path ingestion outside these roots. Defense-in-depth for prompt-influenced agent calls; the real protection is host-side MCP approval.

size_cap_files.description: Aborts directory ingestion before any work if file count exceeds this. Bypassable via CLI --force; MCP has no force option.

size_cap_bytes.description: Aborts directory ingestion before any work if total bytes exceed this (default 500 MB). Same bypass model as the file cap.

offline_mode.description: When set, blocks web fetch and Hugging Face GGUF download. Localhost ollama remains available.

safety.models_cache_path: ~/.cairn/models (embedded runtime only; can be pre-populated for CAIRN_OFFLINE deployments)

supply_chain.package_pin: cairn-index@1.2.1 (exact, no caret/range in clawhub install entry)
supply_chain.model_checksums_published: docs/setup.md → 'Verifying pre-cached models'. SHA256s for nomic-embed-text-v1.5.Q8_0.gguf and Qwen3-0.6B-UD-Q8_K_XL.gguf are pinned to the bytes cairn was developed against.

startup_warnings.cairn_mcp_no_allowlist: On boot, cairn-mcp logs a warning line when CAIRN_ALLOWED_ROOTS is unset, surfacing the path-allowlist call to operators who didn't read the docs.

developer_default.description: Single-user laptop, ollama running locally. No env vars needed.

mcp_agent_curated.description: Confine MCP-driven ingestion to a known tree, with sensible size caps. Belt + suspenders to host-side approval.

air_gapped.description: Zero network egress. Pre-cache GGUFs on a connected machine, copy ~/.cairn/models to the air-gapped host, then run.

schema.fk_cascade_chain: sources → files → chunks; sources → entities → edges/entity_tags
schema.soft_delete: entities only (removed_at IS NULL filter on read); active-entity join hides edges of soft-deleted endpoints
schema.additive_upgrade: v1 → v1.1 picks up entity_tags via CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS on next open; v1.2 added no schema changes (safety gates only)

covers: tree-sitter entity extraction (rust / typescript / tsx / python)
doc-extract LLM pass with hash-gated rerun
tag CRUD (replace, getByTag, getForMany, listTags) + LLM tag emission
BFS shortest-path with batched layer fetch
v1.2 safety gates: CAIRN_OFFLINE, CAIRN_ALLOWED_ROOTS (multi-root, trailing-slash, per-kind), size caps + force bypass

model_validation.rationale: Sub-1GB Q8. Stable structured output via JSON-Schema-enforced decoding (ollama format / llama.cpp grammar). Prompt architecture engineered specifically for this model — bigger ≠ drop-in replacement.
